---
title: "Available workflows"
description: "Every workflow OpenPipeline offers, arranged by where it sits in the pipeline."
order: 60
---

OpenPipeline takes single-cell and spatial data from raw sequencing output to a
batch-corrected, clustered, annotated dataset. Both paths follow the same shape:
ingestion, per-sample processing (filtering and QC before normalization, with
thresholds set from a QC report), integration, and downstream analysis. The
spatial path uses spatial-aware ingestion and adds spatial-specific downstream
analysis. Switch between the two paths below.

```{=html}
<div class="op-home"><div id="op-flow" data-layout="vertical" data-detailed="true"></div></div>
<script>window.FLOW_BASE = '../';</script>
<script src="../flow.js"></script>
```

## Demultiplexing

If your sequencing provider delivers raw Illumina BCL files, convert them to
per-sample FASTQ files at the start of the pipeline. This step is **often already
performed upstream** by your sequencing provider, but is also available within
OpenPipeline.

The `ingestion/demux` workflow supports three demultiplexers selected via
`--demultiplexer`:

- **bcl2fastq**: legacy Illumina tool
- **BCL Convert**: current Illumina general-purpose demultiplexer
- **cellranger mkfastq**: 10x Genomics wrapper around BCL Convert with
  convenience features for 10x libraries

If you already have FASTQ files or a pre-built count matrix, skip directly to
[Ingestion](#ingestion).

| Workflow | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`ingestion/demux`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/demux.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | BCL → FASTQ demultiplexing (bcl2fastq, BCL Convert, mkfastq) |

## Ingestion

Every cell in a single-cell experiment is tagged with a unique barcode before
sequencing. Ingestion aligns the resulting short reads back to a reference
genome, uses the barcode to assign each read to a cell, and uses the UMI (Unique
Molecular Identifier) to count each RNA molecule exactly once, removing the
amplification bias from PCR. The result is a **count matrix**: cells × genes,
with each entry being the number of RNA molecules detected.

OpenPipeline stores this count matrix, along with cell and gene metadata and any
additional modalities (ADT, VDJ, ATAC), in a single **H5MU file per sample**.
This per-sample H5MU is the unit that flows through all subsequent steps. The
right ingestion workflow depends on your sequencing platform and library type.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Guides:** [Ingestion](../guides/ingest-10x.qmd) covers workflow selection,
reference genome setup, and examples; [Spatial ingestion](../guides/ingest-spatial.qmd)
covers the spatial platforms.
:::

| Workflow / component | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`ingestion/make_reference`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/make_reference.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Build a genome reference index for mapping |
| [`ingestion/cellranger_mapping`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_mapping.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | 10x GEX-only mapping with Cell Ranger |
| [`ingestion/cellranger_multi`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_multi.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | 10x multiome mapping (GEX + ADT/HTO + VDJ) with Cell Ranger Multi |
| [`ingestion/bd_rhapsody`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/ingestion/bd_rhapsody.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | BD Rhapsody mapping |
| [`ingestion/spaceranger_mapping`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/ingestion/spaceranger_mapping.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | Space Ranger mapping for Visium spatial data |
| [`convert/from_spaceranger_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_spaceranger_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | Visium (Space Ranger) output → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | 10x Xenium output → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_cosmx_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_cosmx_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | NanoString CosMx output → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | Element AVITI (cells2stats) output → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_spatialdata_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_spatialdata_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | SpatialData object → H5MU |

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Already have a count matrix, but not in H5MU?"}
If your data is already quantified but stored in another format (AnnData, Seurat,
10x HDF5/MEX, TileDB), convert it to H5MU with one of these components:

| Component | Package | Converts |
|---|---|---|
| [`convert/from_cellranger_multi_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_cellranger_multi_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Cell Ranger `multi` output → H5MU (preferred: keeps full metadata) |
| [`convert/from_10xmtx_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_10xmtx_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | 10x MEX (MTX) → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_h5ad_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_h5ad_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | AnnData (.h5ad) → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_seurat_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_seurat_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Seurat → H5MU |
| [`convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/convert/from_tiledb_to_h5mu.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | TileDB → H5MU |

The reverse converters (H5MU → AnnData, Seurat, TileDB) exist too, for exporting
results back out to other ecosystems.
:::

## QC report

A QC report computes the key QC metrics up front (counts, detected features, and
mitochondrial and ribosomal fractions) so you can choose sensible filtering
thresholds. A spatial-specific version is available too.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Guides:** [QC report](../guides/qc-report.qmd) and
[Spatial QC report](../guides/qc-report-spatial.qmd).
:::

| Workflow | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`generate_qc_report`](../reference/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/workflows/generate_qc_report.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_qc"></span>`openpipeline_qc`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_qc} | Key QC metrics rendered as a report |
| [`qc/spatial_qc`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/qc/spatial_qc.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | QC report for spatial data |

## Process samples

Raw count matrices contain not only real cells but also empty or ambient-RNA
droplets, damaged cells, and (for droplet-based methods) doublets. Ambient RNA can
optionally be removed up front with the stand-alone `cellbender_remove_background`
step. Two end-to-end workflows then handle all per-sample and multi-sample
processing in a single command, and are the only ones most users need to run
directly:

| Workflow / component | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`multiomics/process_samples`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/multiomics/process_samples.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | End-to-end processing for single-cell data |
| [`multiomics/spatial_process_samples`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/multiomics/spatial_process_samples.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | End-to-end processing for spatial data |
| [`correction/cellbender_remove_background`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/correction/cellbender_remove_background.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Optional stand-alone step: remove ambient RNA from raw counts |

`process_samples` runs the whole chain in one command. Each sample is first
filtered on its own distribution (count, quantile, feature, and
mitochondrial/ribosomal thresholds, plus Scrublet doublet detection); all samples
are then concatenated and processed together, normalization, highly variable gene
selection, and PCA, because those cohort steps need to see the full range of
variation across samples. The [Sample processing](../guides/process-samples.qmd)
and [Spatial sample processing](../guides/process-spatial.qmd) guides cover the
filtering strategies, the per-sample-then-cohort sequence, and threshold choices
in detail.

:::{.callout-note}
ATAC data is carried through `process_samples` without dedicated QC or
normalization, ATAC-specific processing workflows are not yet implemented. ATAC
ingestion (`ingestion/cellranger_atac_count`) and a standalone QC metrics module
(`calculate_atac_qc_metrics`) are available.
:::

## Integration

When samples come from different donors, sequencing runs, or library
preparations, cells of the same biological type will cluster by their technical
origin rather than their biology. **Batch correction** realigns these cells in
the PCA embedding so that biological similarity, not technical source, drives the
cell groupings.

From the corrected embedding, a **k-nearest-neighbor graph** is built: each cell
is connected to its most similar neighbours. Leiden community detection runs on
this graph to assign **cluster labels**, and UMAP projects the graph into 2D for
visualization. These three outputs (corrected embedding, cluster labels, UMAP)
are what annotation and downstream tools consume.

OpenPipeline provides one workflow per integration method. There is no single
default; the right choice depends on dataset size and batch structure.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Guide:** [Integration](../guides/integrate.qmd): guidance on choosing a method.
:::

| Workflow | Package | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`integration/harmony_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/harmony_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Harmony | Iterative linear correction of the PCA embedding |
| [`integration/scvi_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/scvi_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | scVI | Variational autoencoder that learns an integrated latent space |
| [`integration/scanorama_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/scanorama_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Scanorama | Mutual-nearest-neighbor integration (panorama stitching) |
| [`integration/bbknn_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/bbknn_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | BBKNN | Batch-balanced neighbor graph; produces no corrected embedding |
| [`integration/totalvi_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/totalvi_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | totalVI | Joint RNA + protein (CITE-seq) model; corrects both modalities |
| [`single_cell/parallel_integration`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_integration.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_composed"></span>`openpipeline_composed`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_composed} | multiple | Runs several of the above methods on the same input and merges their outputs |

## Downstream

After integration, the numbered Leiden clusters have no biological labels yet.
**Annotation** assigns cell type identities. In OpenPipeline this is always
reference-based, transferring labels from an in-house trained model, an
open-source reference model, or a reference dataset. This step is always run
separately from the core pipeline and depends on the tissue and biological
question.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Guide:** [Cell type annotation](../guides/annotate.qmd): details and examples.
:::

| Workflow | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`annotation/harmony_knn`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/harmony_knn.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | KNN label transfer from a reference atlas |
| [`annotation/scvi_knn`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/scvi_knn.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | KNN label transfer using scVI latent space |
| [`annotation/scanvi_scarches`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/scanvi_scarches.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | scANVI/scArches label transfer |
| [`annotation/celltypist`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/celltypist.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | CellTypist marker-based annotation |
| [`single_cell/parallel_annotation`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_annotation.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_composed"></span>`openpipeline_composed`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_composed} | Run several annotation methods at once and take a consensus vote across them |

Beyond annotation, the integrated object is the starting point for a range of
downstream analyses, available as OpenPipeline workflows or components:

| Workflow / component | Package | Description |
|---|---|---|
| [`niche/nichecompass_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/niche/nichecompass_leiden.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial} | NicheCompass niche identification |
| [`velocity/scvelo`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/velocity/scvelo.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | RNA velocity (scVelo) |
| [`interpret/lianapy`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/interpret/lianapy.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Cell-cell communication (LIANA) |
| [`differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2.qmd) | [<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline} | Pseudobulk differential expression (DESeq2) |

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Guides:** [Differential expression](../guides/differential-expression.qmd),
[RNA velocity](../guides/rna-velocity.qmd),
[Cell-cell communication](../guides/cell-cell-communication.qmd), and
[Spatial niches](../guides/spatial-niches.qmd).
:::
